You are a developer migrating from Git to `jj` (Jujutsu), working in a repository at `/home/user/project`.

The repository has the following structure:
- `main` bookmark points to a commit described as `chore: project setup` (creates `main.py` with content `print('main')`)
- From the `root()`, there is also a separate line of commits:
  - `feat: add helper` (creates `helper.py` with content `def helper(): pass`)
  - Your working copy `@` is at `feat: add helper`

The `main` bookmark was updated by a teammate after you created `feat: add helper`. As a result, your feature commit is currently based on `root()`, not on `main`.

You need to rebase your working copy commit (`feat: add helper`) so that it is based on the commit pointed to by `main` (i.e., `chore: project setup`). Do not create any new commits.

After rebasing, save the output of `jj log --no-graph -r 'root()..@' -T 'description ++ "\n"'` to `/home/user/project/rebase.log`.

Your task is complete when:
- The parent of `@` is the commit described as `chore: project setup`
- `feat: add helper` is the description of `@`
- Both `main.py` and `helper.py` exist in the working directory
- `/home/user/project/rebase.log` exists and contains both `chore: project setup` and `feat: add helper`

**Analyzing Rebase Scenario**

I'm currently focused on the rebase operation using `jj` in the `/home/user/project` repository. The `main` bookmark is pointing to the initial setup commit. The working copy is ready. My next step is to prepare the rebase operation by identifying the target branch and the commits to be rebased.


**Planning the Jujutsu Rebase**

I've examined the setup: I'm in the `/home/user/project` repository with the working copy at `feat: add helper` and `main` at the initial commit. My plan is to run `jj rebase -r @ -d main` to move the feature branch onto `main`. Then, I'll verify the parentage and the file existence. Finally, I will extract and save the `jj log` output to `/home/user/project/rebase.log`.

results.content:
Move revisions to different parent(s)

Usage: jj rebase [OPTIONS] <--onto <REVSETS>|--insert-after <REVSETS>|--insert-before <REVSETS>>

Options:
  -b, --branch <REVSETS>         Rebase the whole branch relative to destination's ancestors (can be
                                 repeated)
  -s, --source <REVSETS>         Rebase specified revision(s) together with their trees of
                                 descendants (can be repeated)
  -r, --revisions <REVSETS>      Rebase the given revisions, rebasing descendants onto this
                                 revision's parent(s)
  -o, --onto <REVSETS>           The revision(s) to rebase onto (can be repeated to create a merge
                                 commit) [aliases: -d]
  -A, --insert-after <REVSETS>   The revision(s) to insert after (can be repeated to create a merge
                                 commit) [aliases: --after]
  -B, --insert-before <REVSETS>  The revision(s) to insert before (can be repeated to create a merge
                                 commit) [aliases: --before]
      --skip-emptied             If true, when rebasing would produce an empty commit, the commit is
                                 abandoned. It will not be abandoned if it was already empty before
                                 the rebase. Will never skip merge commits with multiple non-empty
                                 parents
      --keep-divergent           Keep divergent commits while rebasing
  -h, --help                     Print help (see more with '--help')

Global Options:
  -R, --repository <REPOSITORY>      Path to repository to operate on
      --ignore-working-copy          Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it
      --ignore-immutable             Allow rewriting immutable commits
      --at-operation <AT_OPERATION>  Operation to load the repo at [aliases: --at-op]
      --debug                        Enable debug logging
      --color <WHEN>                 When to colorize output [possible values: always, never, debug,
                                     auto]
      --quiet                        Silence non-primary command output
      --no-pager                     Disable the pager
      --config <NAME=VALUE>          Additional configuration options (can be repeated)
      --config-file <PATH>           Additional configuration files (can be repeated)
